comment: AI-generated review; verified tests, required checks, and human approval remain merge gates.
instructions: Review for exploitable security, authorization, correctness, data-integrity, concurrency, and production-readiness defects. Require file and line evidence. Avoid style-only comments and never treat generated prose as proof of testing.

rules.rule: EVIDENCE: report only actionable defects with file:line evidence, a concrete failure mode, and a concise remediation. Do not report formatting or preference-only issues.
AUTHZ: server-side mutations must derive identity from a verified session and re-check ownership or role. Flag trust in client-supplied identities, roles, tenant IDs, or resource ownership.
SECRETS: flag hardcoded credentials, private keys, tokens, cookies, secrets in logs, and server-only values exposed to clients. Example values must be unmistakable placeholders.
FAIL-CLOSED: authentication, authorization, internal tools, deployment gates, and security controls must deny by default when configuration is missing or invalid.
INPUT: validate and bound untrusted input at the boundary. Flag unsafe parsing, path traversal, injection, NaN/Infinity, unbounded payloads, and missing schema validation.
IDEMPOTENCY: webhooks, jobs, imports, billing, settlement, and retryable handlers must not duplicate irreversible effects when replayed.
CONCURRENCY: flag read-then-write races, missing transactions or locks, unsafe shared state, non-atomic ledger/balance changes, and duplicate worker execution.
DEPENDENCIES: flag unpinned executable downloads, unsafe install hooks, dependency-confusion exposure, and upgrades that violate runtime or lockfile contracts.
TESTS: behavior changes need regression coverage at the failure boundary. Flag tests that mock away the critical behavior or assertions that cannot fail for the defect.
WEB RUNTIME: keep server-only modules and secrets out of client bundles; flag unsafe dynamic execution, missing await/error handling, and route handlers that trust client state.
WEB BOUNDARIES: authenticate before parsing privileged operations, enforce CSRF/origin controls where cookies authorize writes, validate request bodies, and return non-sensitive errors.
BROWSER SECURITY: flag XSS sinks, unsafe HTML injection, untrusted redirects, token storage in browser-accessible persistence, and links/windows missing appropriate isolation.
DEPLOYMENT: flag public exposure of internal routes or artifacts, missing security headers, preview/production drift, unsafe environment fallbacks, and deployments not tied to the reviewed commit.
